GOVERN~1ENTAL EDUCATION AND RESEARCH 
P.O. BOX 1293 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K Street 
Sacramento, California 95814 
Re: 
GEAR 
Dear Si rs, 
SANTA CRUZ, Ca., 95061 
Pursuant to Section 83114 of the California Government Code, the Governmental 
E'ducation and Research Association and N. Manfred Shaffer, its executive direc-
tor, and a member of the association, hereby request an opinion as to whether it 
or its members are required to file campaign statements or a statement of organi-
sation or both as a recipient committee, expenditure committee, or major donor 
committee under its Political Reform Act of 1974. 
Governmental Education and Research is a non-profit association of business 
and professional people in Santa Cruz County. 
The association was formed for the 
purposes set forth in the Articles of Association, a copy of which is attached. 
The purposes are generally to study local government issues, to report upon such 
issues to the members of the association, to formulate positions on such issues, 
and to inform the public of its position on such issues. 
There was some recent 
discussion of the possibility of providing services to local candidates, but it 
vIas determi ned not to do thi s. 
It is anticipated that the annual budget for the association will be in ex-
cess of $5,000 per year. 
Copies of a promotional brochure and a proposed newsletter which further 
describe the intent of the association are attached hereto to indicate the con-
templated activities of the association. 
It is specifically intended that the representatives of the association 
will attend meetings of government bodies, conduct research into various gov-
ernmental actions, compile 
information based on the research, and disclose the 
information to its members and to the general public. 
As noted above, your opinion is requested as to whether the association 
is, assuming that its actions are consistent with the aforementioned intent, 
required to file campaign statements or a statement of organisation or both. 
If any additional information is required, please feel free to contact me at 
the following address: P.O. BOX 1293, Santa Cruz, Ca., 95061. 
JG/pd 
enc. 
Sincerely yours, 
4;.~¥ 
N. Manfred Shaffer

What is Governmental Education and Research (GEAR)? 
Since this is our first newsletter, we want to explain a bit about 
our goals and objectives and what we hope to accomplish in the coming months. 
If we are to succeed in our efforts to have local Government which is 
responsible to all the residents of Santa Cruz, we cannot rely on the me-
thods that we have used in the past. 
The "far left" and socialist fa.ct~ 
10ns in their desire to control this City and County have developed very 
sophisticated and well organized strategies. 
If we are going to make our 
vie\tls kXlOwn:, 
it is essential that we develop our own well organized ef-
forts. 
GEAR is the vehicle by which we can accomplish this. 
With your sup-
port, GEAR will make every effort to ensure that all governmental offices 
are held by individuals who are responsive to the community needs. 
GEAR intends to provide a wide variety of services, including: 
* development and analysis of key issues and programs as they relate to 
Santa Cruz 
* a regular newsletter analyzing current political and economic affairs 
* 
* 
* 
seminars to infonn members of a wide variety of issues related to re .. 
sponsible government 
voter registration profiles 
identification and encouragement of as many volunteers as possible 
to participate in the political process 
* conununi cat; on of the concerns of our membershi p to a 11 candi dates 
* AND a continuing effort to keep all officeholders aware of the needs 
of our members

GEAR is committed to an educational 
process, which will seek to inform all 
residents, 
particularly 
its 
members 
of 
proposed and pending legislation which 
could adversely affect or enhance its 
freedoms. A regular newsletter-GEAR-
UP-will provide members with an update 
of local political and economic issues. 
Members may be provided with a wide 
variety of services to aid in the organizing 
and directing of an individual's participation 
in the governmental process. 
Seminars will 
be' conducted to make 
members aware of the mechanics for 
effective political participation. 
MEMBERSHIP INFORMATION 
Any person or organization is eligible to 
apply for membership in GEAR. A member-
ship committee reviews all applications 
and makes recommendations to the Board 
of Directors. Four types of membership 
have been established-
Sponsor 
Business 
Patron 
Subscriber (Individuals) 
We invite you to participate. 
Initial 
. Fee

500

Renewal

Governmental Education and Research 
147 River St. South 
Santa Cruz, Ca. 95060 
Are you concerned that qualified people 
who can represent you are encouraged to 
become 
candidates 
for 
local 
political 
offices? Are you concerned that they be 
provided with all of the tools necessary to 
ensure success in their efforts to win public 
office? And are you concerned that they 
continue to receive the advice and viewpoints 
of people such as yourself? If so-you are 
encouraged to participate in the activities 
of GEAR. 
GEAR has been formed to meet an unfulfilled 
need in Santa Cruz for effective awareness 
and action programs. The time for talk is 
past-the time for action is now. 
Our local city council and board of super-
visors are the major molding forces which 
foster an economic and cultural climate for 
our basic political, economic and social 
institutions to flourish. For this reason~ it is 
imperative that every business and profes-
sional 
person accept responsibility for 
ensuring that policy making and service in 
official positions of trust is in the hands of 
individuals dedicated to the preservation 
of democratic principles.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Governmental Education and Research Association (GEAR), a non-profit association focused on studying local government issues and informing the public, must file campaign statements or statements of organization under the Political Reform Act of 1974.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request document, not an FPPC response/advice letter. No CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section present. Document includes supporting materials (Articles of Association, promotional brochure, newsletter excerpt). The 'old' format note indicates this is from 1981 and may have formatting variations.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from N. Manfred Shaffer, executive director of GEAR, a non-profit association in Santa Cruz County, asking the FPPC for an opinion on whether the association is required to file campaign statements or statements of organization under the Political Reform Act of 1974. The letter includes the association's Articles of Association and promotional materials describing GEAR's purposes and intended activities.
